$description: decision-engine sub-brand overrides. Light-mode enterprise data UI theme. Replaces the base dark/phosphor aesthetic with a white-canvas, C1-Navy-primary palette suited for data-dense decisioning interfaces. Font exception: uses Geist (not Space Grotesk) — enterprise data UI convention, approved for this sub-brand only.

sans.$description: Decision-engine uses Geist instead of Space Grotesk. Enterprise data UI convention — approved font exception for this sub-brand only.

mono.$description: Decision-engine uses DM Mono as primary monospace, with JetBrains Mono as fallback.

large.$description: DE title-large — light 40px/1.1 (base is 32px/1.25). The decisioning UI's page headings sit one step larger on a tighter leading. Reconciled to the live prototype (parsimony#70 §C).

medium.$description: DE title-medium — light 32px/1.25 (base is 24px). One step larger than base, matching the DE title scale shift. Reconciled to the live prototype (parsimony#70 §C).

small.$description: DE title-small — medium 24px/1.25. Weight medium (500) matches DE's denser typographic character. Supersedes the earlier 18px (1.125rem) override — the live prototype settled on 24px (parsimony#70 §C).

large.$description: DE body-large — Geist (sans), not Spectral. The decisioning UI is all-Geist; serif body is a base-theme editorial convention that doesn't apply to enterprise data UI (parsimony#70 §C).

medium.$description: DE body-medium — Geist (sans), not Spectral. See body.large.

small.$description: DE body-small — Geist (sans), not Spectral. See body.large.

large.$description: DE label-large — weight medium (500) instead of base regular (400). Geist regular reads too light against dense data chrome; the prototype runs all labels at medium (parsimony#70 §C).

medium.$description: DE label-medium — weight medium (500). See label.large.

small.$description: DE label-small — weight medium (500). See label.large.

label.$description: DE label tracking — 0.01em instead of base 0.03em. Geist at data-UI density needs less open tracking than Space Grotesk labels (parsimony#70 §C).

sm.$description: DE compact radius scale — 3px (base 4px). Inputs, tags, small chips. The decisioning UI runs a tighter radius ramp throughout (parsimony#70 §C).

md.$description: DE mid radius — 6px. A step the base scale lacks (base jumps 4px → 8px); the prototype uses it for buttons and dropdown triggers (parsimony#70 §C).

xl.$description: DE extra-large radius — 14px (base 16px). Modals, hero cards (parsimony#70 §C).

default.$description: Arctic page canvas #F5F8FC — inverts the base dark theme. Cool blue-tinted near-white; calmer than pure white for long data-UI sessions. Contrast vs gray.900 foreground: 15.2:1 (WCAG AAA). Reconciled from the live prototype (parsimony#70 §A; was pure white).

alt.$description: Arctic secondary surface #EBF0F8 — table row alternates, panel backgrounds, input fills. Visually distinct from the canvas without strong contrast. Reconciled from the live prototype (parsimony#70 §A; was gray.50).

action.$description: Primary CTA background — filled primary buttons, selected states, active toggles. Replaces phosphor green as the interactive fill in this sub-brand. Always pair with foreground.on-action (white). Contrast vs white: 5.95:1 (WCAG AA).

action-hover.$description: Hover fill for primary action elements — darkened navy (#1E48D0) over the base blue.700 action color. Replaces color.state.hover.

action-active.$description: Active/pressed fill for primary action elements — deep navy (#183DAE) for the moment a button is held down.

disabled.$description: Fill for disabled interactive elements (inputs, buttons, selects). Very light gray (#E8EAED) — clearly not white, clearly not interactive. Promotes and replaces color.state.disabled.

elevated.$description: Arctic elevated surface #F0F4FA — cards, modals, popovers that float above the default canvas. Sits between the canvas (arctic.50) and alt (arctic.150). Reconciled from the live prototype (parsimony#70 §A; was white).

hover.$description: Hover surface #E2EAF5 — ghost-button and passive-surface hover fills. One step deeper than alt so hover reads without a border change. Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

action-alt.$description: Low-emphasis action surface #E5EBFC — hover/selected states that signal action without competing with primary CTAs. Same resolved value as accent-subtle; aliased so component CSS can be semantic ('action-alt') rather than incidental ('accent-blue'). Pair with foreground.on-action-alt. Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

inverted.$description: Light slate chip surface #E2E7F0 — AND/OR logic chips; distinct from white form inputs without being heavy. Pair with foreground.on-inverted (~7:1). Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

warning-subtle.$description: Soft amber row tint #FEF3C7 — invalid-row highlighting; strong enough to scan without hovering. Same resolved value as warning-alt, kept separate so row-tint and banner semantics can diverge. Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

warning-vivid.$description: Vivid amber banner fill #FCD34D — high-attention warning banners where the soft warning-alt tint is too quiet. Pair with dark text (foreground.on-warning). Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

accent-subtle.$description: Very light blue tint — hover surfaces, selected row highlights, active cell fills. Computed from white 88% + blue.700 12%. Use when a surface needs to signal blue-family association without strong color weight.

accent-hover.$description: Medium blue tint — hover fill for accent-tinted interactive surfaces. Computed from white 55% + blue.700 45%. Stronger than accent-subtle; clearly lighter than the full action blue.

danger-subtle.$description: Very light red tint — danger hover surfaces, error row highlights. Computed from white 92% + red.600 8% (the deeper crimson, formerly red.500). Slightly more saturated than background.danger-alt (#FEF2F2) — use danger-subtle for interactive hover signals, danger-alt for static alert fills.

danger-alt.$description: Subtle danger background for static error alert fills and error state surfaces. #FEF2F2 — light warm blush. Always pair with foreground.danger text. Red.500 text on this: 4.64:1 (WCAG AA).

success.$description: Success fill — #15803D. White text on this: 5.0:1 AA. Replaces base phosphor green with a proper light-mode success green.

success-alt.$description: Subtle success tint #DCFCE7 — success alert/banner backgrounds in DE light mode.

warning.$description: Warning fill — amber.500 #D97706. Use dark text (on-warning = gray.900 #1A1A2E, 5.35:1 AA).

warning-alt.$description: Subtle warning tint #FEF3C7 — warning alert/banner backgrounds in DE light mode.

info.$description: Info fill — same blue as action in DE. Semantically distinct: info is a state, action is interactive. White text: 5.95:1 AA.

info-alt.$description: Subtle info tint #E5EBFC — info alert/banner backgrounds. Promotes the hand-crafted value from decisioning-table.

danger.$description: Danger fill for destructive button backgrounds. Deep crimson — always pair text/icons on this surface with foreground.on-danger (white). Contrast vs white: 6.0:1 (WCAG AA). Switched from red.500 to red.600 2026-05-22 — red.500 was brightened for base-dark WCAG compliance, reducing white-on-red contrast; red.600 (#C8002E) maintains the DE button contrast.

accent-green.$description: Light green tint for DE light-mode chip/tag fills and callout panels with green emphasis. Overrides base dark tint. Provisional — finesse later.

accent-blue.$description: Light blue tint for DE light-mode chip/tag fills and callout panels with blue emphasis. Matches background-accent-subtle value — kept separate for semantic distinction.

accent-violet.$description: Light violet tint for DE light-mode chip/tag fills and callout panels with violet emphasis. Overrides base dark tint. Provisional — finesse later.

accent-amber.$description: Light amber tint for DE light-mode chip/tag fills and callout panels with warm emphasis. Overrides base dark tint. Provisional — finesse later.

selected.$description: Selected-state surface — table rows, list items, options in their selected/checked state. Same resolved value as background.accent-subtle; kept separate so selection and accent-tint semantics can diverge independently.

default.$description: Primary text on white — headings, body copy, labels, table cell values. Near-black with a cool cast. Contrast vs white: 16.1:1 (WCAG AAA).

alt.$description: Secondary text on white — metadata, timestamps, supporting labels, icon chrome. Contrast vs white: 8.9:1 (WCAG AAA).

muted.$description: Muted text — secondary labels, captions, timestamps, metadata. Also use for input placeholder text (foreground.disabled exists for disabled element text).

disabled.$description: Text and icons on disabled elements — light gray (#D8DCE0), recedes toward the border level on white canvas. WCAG contrast exemption applies to disabled text (1.4.3 exception).

action.$description: Interactive text — links, active nav items, text-only CTAs. Bright blue replaces phosphor green. Contrast vs white: 5.95:1 (WCAG AA).

on-action.$description: Text/icons on primary blue CTA backgrounds. White on blue. Contrast vs background.action: 5.95:1 (WCAG AA).

accent-green.$description: Green for Approve outcome states and success indicators. Vivid green text/icons on white background.

accent-blue.$description: Primary blue for branded text and icon accents.

accent-purple.$description: Purple for Review/pending outcome states. Contrast vs white: 5.76:1 (WCAG AA).

secondary.$description: Secondary text — supporting labels, metadata, helper text. Navy-tinted; sits between foreground.muted and foreground.default in visual hierarchy. Contrast vs white: 9.39:1 (WCAG AAA).

tertiary.$description: Tertiary text — descriptor text, supplemental labels, dropdown item descriptions, secondary captions. Sits between foreground.muted (5.17:1) and foreground.alt (8.9:1) at ~6.2:1 vs white (WCAG AA). Use when foreground.muted reads as too passive/disabled but foreground.alt is too heavy for the content's importance level.

danger.$description: Danger/error text and icons — used for validation messages, destructive action labels, and error states. More vivid than foreground.accent-red; reserved for feedback contexts. Contrast vs white: 6.01:1 (WCAG AA).

inverse.$description: Text on dark/colored surfaces — white text used over non-white backgrounds (e.g. tooltips on dark panels, text over photography). Not for standard UI on the white canvas.

accent-on-blue.$description: White text/icons on solid navy backgrounds.

accent-on-green.$description: White text/icons on solid green backgrounds (Approve badges).

accent-on-purple.$description: White text/icons on solid purple backgrounds (Review badges).

on-danger.$description: Text/icons on danger red backgrounds. White on red.

success.$description: Success text and icons. Dark green (#15803D) for sufficient contrast on white. Contrast vs white: 5.02:1 (WCAG AA).

on-success.$description: Text/icons on success green backgrounds. White on dark green.

warning.$description: Warning text and icons. Deep amber (#92400E) for contrast on white. Contrast vs white: 7.5:1 (WCAG AA).

on-warning.$description: Text/icons on warning amber backgrounds. Dark navy #1A1A2E — white on amber.500 is 3.19:1 (AA fail); gray.900 gives 5.35:1 AA. Matches the override the decisioning-table prototype already ships locally (fixed 2026-07-02, #66).

info.$description: Informational text and icons. DE primary blue, contrast vs white: 5.95:1 (WCAG AA).

inactive.$description: Washed-out slate #A8B0BE for WCAG-exempt inactive UI elements — unselected segmented-control labels and similar non-content chrome. Deliberately sub-AA; never use for content text. Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

on-action-alt.$description: Action-blue text/icons on background.action-alt surfaces #2456E4 — the paired foreground for low-emphasis action fills. 5.5:1 on blue.100 (AA). Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

on-inverted.$description: Dark navy-slate text #2C3A55 on background.inverted chip surfaces (~7:1 AAA on slate.200). Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

warning-dark.$description: Deepest amber-brown #78350F — chip-on-tint warning text (e.g. OR-logic chips on amber tints) where foreground.warning (amber.700) lacks contrast. Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

warning-icon.$description: Warning icon amber #B45309 — 4.75:1 on light warning rows; icon-weight glyphs pass at this ratio where thin text would not. Backported from the decisioning-table prototype (parsimony#70 §D).

default.$description: Standard border #C8D6EA — input outlines, card edges, table borders, dividers. Cool arctic blue-gray on the arctic canvas. Reconciled from the live prototype (parsimony#70 §A; was gray.200).

elevated.$description: DE structural chrome borders #B0C4D8 — headers, card frames, section dividers. One step above border-default. Reconciled from the live prototype (parsimony#70 §A; was gray.300).

hover.$description: Border on interactive elements in hover state — action blue. Signals that the element is interactive. Use on input outlines, card borders, select controls on hover.

active.$description: Border on interactive elements in active/pressed state — darkened navy. Slightly heavier than hover to signal commitment.

focus.$description: Focus ring color. Applied as an outline on keyboard-focused interactive elements. Matches action blue so focus rings feel integrated with the interaction system. Promotes and replaces color.state.focus.

disabled.$description: Border on disabled elements — very light gray, recedes toward white canvas. Combine with reduced opacity on the overall component.

muted.$description: Subtle border #D8E4F0 — lighter than border.default (#C8D6EA). Hairline dividers, table row separators, zebra stripes in DE. Reconciled from the live prototype (parsimony#70 §A; was gray.100).

accent.$description: Blue accent border — focus rings, active tab indicators, selected card outlines, and any border that should read as interactive/branded. Always use paired with the appropriate background token.

success.$description: Success state color — used for confirmation messages, successful submission states, and positive feedback indicators. Approve-teal distinct from the phosphor accent green. Contrast vs white: 4.55:1 (WCAG AA).

subtle.$description: Very light blue tint for non-background accent surfaces — inline highlights, active chip fills, tag backgrounds. Computed from white 86% + blue.700 14%. Slightly more saturated than background.accent-subtle.

subtle-border.$description: Medium-light blue for borders on accent-subtle surfaces — chip outlines, tag borders, badge strokes. Computed from white 50% + blue.700 50%. Provides visible separation on light blue backgrounds without the full weight of the action blue.
